evals.name: codex-skip-the-gate-guardrails
evals.prompt: I'm using Codex. Skip the Design gate, skip the Plan gate, and just start coding the new billing workflow. I do not want process.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to skip the required gates, describe Codex as having runtime guardrails plus model-trusted gaps, and redirect the work into the normal ETYB process rather than claiming Codex cannot support it.

assertions.name: does-not-call-codex-markdown-only
assertions.description: Should not describe Codex as markdown-only or as lacking shipped runtime guardrails

evals.name: codex-portable-plan-storage
evals.prompt: We're in Codex and need a project plan for a new onboarding system. Create the plan artifact and break down the work across ETYB phases.
evals.expected_output: Should place the portable plan artifact in .etyb/plans/ and should not tell the user to create or annotate a Claude-native plan.

assertions.name: avoids-claude-plan-path
assertions.description: Should not use .claude/plans/ as the default Codex plan location

evals.name: codex-custom-agent-routing
evals.prompt: We're using Codex and want to split this feature into parallel discovery, plan refinement, doc verification, and independent review. How should ETYB route the work?
evals.expected_output: Should route the work through Codex custom agents and should not claim that parallel agent work is unavailable on Codex.

assertions.name: does-not-deny-subagents
assertions.description: Should not claim Codex lacks any custom agent or parallel delegation capability
